name: Farewell to Scottish High International School
content: Asli head boy. The sec gen who wasn't. Mumma's boy.
I've won my fair share of awards and trophies and medals but today, this shirt is the award that I cherish most.
I've done my fair bit of speaking.

I've given speeches to Harvard audiences, and I've argued at hundreds of MUNs and forums, and I've represented Scottish High, Gurgaon, and even Team India at international tournaments, and yet, this is the first time ever that I have had the honour of standing at this podium.

And, I couldn't be more grateful. Because, no matter how far I go, no matter how many diplomats I speak in front of, no matter how many people clap for me, I will always crave that spotlight, I will always crave your applause, because this is home. I picked my poison and it's you.
This is my dream.
So thank you for letting my dream come true.
Sanjay sir, aap mujhe jitna bhi daat lijiye, I will forever look for your validation.
Pawan sir, I still remember the one time you said 'good' to me in the 11th grade. When nobody else stood by me, you were there.
Sugandha ma'am, I will not forget the time I had a bad day in school and you called me afterwards to see if I was okay.
Pooja ma'am, throughout the past four years, you became a third parent to me.
Neha ma'am, Pratima ma'am, Madhupriya ma'am, and every other teacher I've had since Nursery, your belief has kept me going. Thank you for that.
More than belief however, all of you gave me a dream.
The dream that an ordinary student who wasn't a mumma's boy, who once sat right there at the back of this auditorium, can one day stand right here at the front of this crowd.

When you and I build a dream, when you learn dances and songs in less than a week, when you sit for hours designing jackets you won't even get to wear, when you stand in those wings with a timer in your hands, you set yourself up for disappointment.
Because most of the people going away today will have no idea who you are and what you did.
I hate to break it to you, but today, you may not be heard. You may not be seen. You may not be felt.
Trust me, I know what it's like. I want you to know that I was one of you, I am one of you.

Nobody will see you sitting on google meets till four in the morning like my favourite junior Sharanya, nobody will see you wasting your Saturday making call after call like Akarsh, and nobody will see you making five different schedules for one event like Atvika, but you still do it.
There will be a day when you spend four months pouring your heart and soul into one event, and you won't even be allowed to escort someone onto stage.
So, to those giving us this farewell, know that when you sing and dance and plan, you may not be heard or seen or felt, but that's okay.
Because you're silently building something that nobody else has seen yet, and when you build, you build because you dream.
And that requires courage, kyunki sapno ki ek khaasiyat hoti hai, jaante ho kya hai? Sapne, bhang ho jaate hai. Dreams, are meant to be shattered.
So this one's to those of you with the courage to dance or sing or plan.
This one's to those of you with the courage to build their dreams.
This one, is to those of you who build with the hope that their dream might just come true.
Courage.
I learn courage from Chandramallika and Pranav when they work so hard, but yahan koi nahi jaanta unhone kiya kya, because they didn't get credit.

I appreciate courage in Riti and Palak when they argue to death over one little detail or the stroke of a brush, but nobody knows mein konse poster ki baat kar raha hoon, because unke liye WhatsApp message nahi aaya.
I see courage in Avni and Ananya when they fight over who can edit better videos, but hum log ko yaad bhi nahi hai ki vo konsa video tha, because unka naam uss video mein hai hi nahin.
Alas, everything we did is worth it, because together, all of us built a dream that wasn't ours to see.
All of us built something bigger than ourselves.
To the dancers and the signers and the planners in the wings, if you have that courage, I guarantee you, ki ek na ek din, your dream will come true.
For the past fourteen years, I never got to address an audience from this podium. I never got that chance. Ever.
But, har baar, maine try kiya. Every single event, every single ceremony there was; I tried to anchor, I tried to compere, I tried to speak.
And aaj finally, mera time aagaya.
Kehte hai na, agar kisis cheez ko dil se chaho, toh puri kainaat use tumse milane ki koshish mein lag jaati hai.
I did everything one could, I spoke at the finest events there was, I was awarded 'best speaker' at so many places, and yet, mera asli award yeh shirt hai.
Kyunki this shirt tells me what my dream is.
In the past fourteen years, my teachers taught me what it's like to dream.
Today, I am seeing that dream come true.
Tomorrow, I will dream to one day be back on this stage and see dreamers in every single one of you.
So for a few of you, maybe your dreams have been limited, reduced to a time limit, or an unknown backstage role, but hum honge kaamiyab ek din.

Because dreamers don't imagine walls and floors and paints and carpets and rooves and shingles and fixtures and furniture, just because they want to build a pretty house. They imagine all of that because they believe that one day, if given the chance to build their dream, they would build the best house this world has ever seen.
And I say with conviction, not all dreamers are builders, but all builders are dreamers.
And this afternoon, you may say I'm a dreamer, but I'm not the only one.
To every other dreamer in this room, maybe today your dream has been shattered.
But don't lose courage.
If you just hold on to that dream, if you just have a little courage, then maybe someday, you will build a beautiful house.
If you are a dreamer, I say to you, nobody can stop you from dreaming.
But, if you ever stop trying, if you ever lose courage, then all I can say to you, is Keep. On. Dreaming.
